data.title: Jefferies CEO says bank was “defrauded” by First Brands amid bankruptcy fallout
data.description:
Jefferies dropped a bomb in front of its investors. Rich Handler, the bank’s CEO, came out swinging on Thursday, saying loud and clear: “We believe we were defrauded, OK?” That statement hit as the firm scrambled to explain why it had so much exposure to First Brands Group, the now-bankrupt auto parts supplier. This wasn’t […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jefferies-was-defrauded-by-first-brands/

data.title: New York gold futures hit $4,300, spot silver continues to hit record highs
data.description: Gold futures in New York hit $4,300 per ounce, up 2.34% on the day. Additionally, spot silver reached $54 per ounce, setting a new all-time high and gaining 2% on the day. Silver futures in New York surged 4.00% to $53.46 per ounce. (Gold Ten)

data.title: U.S. GDP Growth Driven by AI, Says White House Crypto Chief
data.description: According to BlockBeats, David Sacks, the White House's head of cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence, known as the 'Crypto Czar,' highlighted that the current U.S. GDP growth rate stands at 3.9%, with artificial intelligence contributing 40% of this growth. Sacks pointed out that while politicians may find it easy to criticize technology for attention, the real question is whether they prefer a 4% growth rate or a 2% growth rate.

data.title: Peter Schiff: Bitcoin has fallen 32% from its August high in terms of gold
data.description: Economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff posted on social media that gold is eating into Bitcoin's lunch. Since its August high, Bitcoin's price in gold terms has fallen 32%. This Bitcoin bear market will be brutal. He recommends Bitcoin holders sell their Bitcoin and buy gold.

data.title: The US government shutdown continues, and the Senate rejects the temporary funding bill for the tenth time
data.description: The U.S. Senate voted 51 to 45 to again fail to advance the Republicans' stopgap funding bill, which would have provided funding for the government until the end of November. This is the 10th consecutive time the Senate has rejected a stopgap funding bill in the past two weeks since the U.S. government shutdown. (Jinshi)

data.title: Dormant Bitcoin Wallet Transfers $694 Million After Three Years
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a Bitcoin wallet identified as 'bc1ql7c' has been activated after three years of inactivity. The wallet recently transferred all 6,500 BTC, valued at approximately $694 million, to a new address. These bitcoins were initially acquired three years ago from Ivanov, also known as Bilyuchenko.

Ivanov, a Russian cryptocurrency entrepreneur, is accused of co-founding the BTC-e and WEX trading platforms. He has also been implicated in laundering hundreds of thousands of bitcoins stolen from the Mt. Gox exchange.

data.title: Ethereum News: ETH Remains Top Destination for Blockchain Developers, Data Confirm
data.description:
Key Insights: New data from Electric Capital suggested that most new crypto developers had turned to the Ethereum ecosystem this year, with Solana and Bitcoin following behind. Between January and September, more than 16,000 developers reportedly joined Ethereum, according to figures shared by the Ethereum Foundation, which cited Electric Capital’s analysis.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/10/17/ethereum-news-eth-remains-top-destination-for-blockchain-developers-data-confirm/

data.title: The ETH long position of "Maji Big Brother" was liquidated again, and the current balance in his account is only $32,800
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, "Brother Maji's" ETH long position was liquidated again, and his current account balance is only $32,800. He experienced a floating profit of $43.64 million to a floating loss of $13 million.

data.title: Bybit Alpha launches DOOD
data.description: Bybit Alpha has newly launched DOOD, which is now open for trading.

data.title: Federal Reserve Highlights Ongoing Economic Impact of Tariffs
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Musalem emphasized that the effects of tariffs continue to permeate the economy, with expectations that this will persist until mid-next year. Retailers are increasingly feeling the pressure to pass on tariff costs, while inflation remains a significant concern for consumers. Musalem stressed the importance of restoring the inflation rate to 2%, expressing full commitment to this target and indicating that the Federal Reserve supports this goal.
